description: We didn't have time to setup and test a proper jail, so this text editor will have to do for now. Can you break free?
name: The text editor jail

description: Can you think of a number which at the same time is one more than itself?
name: An impossible number

description: Can you guess the secret number to win the lottery? The prize is a flag!
name: The flag lottery

description: The webserver for this challenge is storing sensitive data in memory. Can you read it? Did anybody patch since 2014?
name: Sensitive server memory

description: Can you find the bug and trigger an exception in this web application?
name: Try and catch

description: We created a hidden painting which will lead you to the flag. Can you connect the dots and piece it back together?
name: Hidden Painting

description: We have had enough of everybody reading our flags. Since all of our cryptography implementations have been broken, we decided not to roll our own!
name: The encrypted flag

description: We encoded the flag in a terse, but Turing complete programming language. Can you identify the valid characters required to extract the flag?
name: Completely Turing

description: Can you sneak the secret code past the canary hidden down the challenge mine?
name: Flag Canary

description: Our admins take their backup policies very seriously. Every single second they "/bin/tar czf *" our entire home directory. Can you trick the admin's into leaking the flag?
name: Constant Backup Policies

description: We don’t want to share the entire binary, but we will provide a 1-byte memory leak. Can you abuse the leak to gain code execution on the server?
name: Leaky Libraries
